title: quercus humilis f. oxycarpa
intro: Quercus humilis f. oxycarpa, also known as the Hungarian oak, is a subspecies of the dwarf or pedunculate oak (Quercus humilis) and a type of evergreen tree native to Central and Eastern Europe. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine, exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and even have antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus humilis f. oxycarpa include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and act as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, are typically used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
